title: capture card-issuer country + radar score on stripe webhook
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Adds Stripe `charge.succeeded` to the webhook handler so we capture the card-issuer country (BIN), brand, network, and Stripe Radar `risk_level` / `risk_score` on every successful payment. `payment_intent.payment_failed` keeps its existing `paymentIntents.retrieve` so the same fields are captured on declined cards (Stripe doesn't fire `charge.succeeded` on failure).

Card-issuer country is the discrimination point we need for fraud / cohort decisions — independent of IP geo and billing address. Earlier audit work showed near-100% of recent Macao traffic was actually US-issued cards, so this signal lets us tell real geography from spoofed billing.

The credit-grant path (`checkout.session.completed`, `checkout.session.async_payment_succeeded`, `payment_intent.succeeded`) does **not** fetch the Charge — it stays a pure D1 write. Card / Radar enrichment lands via the separate `charge.succeeded` event in Tinybird; dashboards that need card_country query that row directly.

All new data is Tinybird-only. The `stripe_event` datasource gains 10 columns (`card_country`, `card_brand`, `card_network`, `risk_level`, `risk_score`, `presentment_currency`, `presentment_amount`, `payment_method_raw`, `payment_method_wallet`, `payment_methods_offered`). D1 `stripe_checkout_credits` keeps its lean idempotency role and gets no new columns. Also fixes `daily_stripe_revenue` and `paid_customers` to count `checkout.session.async_payment_succeeded` with `payment_status = 'paid'` (the previous filter both missed async-paid customers and incorrectly counted `no_payment_required` setup-only sessions).

End-to-end verified on staging sandbox: real EU-card checkout landed with `card_country=CZ` in Tinybird across all three event types; `pm_card_visa` test landed `card_country=US`. Integration tests cover `charge.succeeded` enriching the Tinybird row and not writing to D1. Tinybird prod schema + pipes deployed via `tb --cloud deploy` (deployment #115).

Required before this works on live:
- En

gist.headline: Radar on the radar
gist.blurb: We've upgraded our Stripe webhook handling to capture card-issuer countries and Radar risk scores. This helps us keep the platform secure and understand our global community better.
gist.summary: Added Stripe charge.succeeded webhook handling to capture card-issuer country, brand, network, and Radar risk scores directly into Tinybird.
gist.impact: This provides internal analytics with accurate geographic data independent of IP or billing address, improving fraud detection and cohort analysis.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot acting like a detective or security guard, inspecting a glowing radar screen alongside the chunky monitor robot, representing the new Stripe Radar and card country tracking.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing a tiny detective hat, inspecting a glowing retro radar screen alongside a chunky robot with a CRT monitor head. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and lavender gradient background. Warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Emotionally warm, nostalgic but beautiful.
